checks.a_analysis: The output content directly corresponds to the text visible in the source PDF. No hallucinated sections or fabricated body text were found. The AI-generated metadata title and image alt text are within acceptable parameters.

All contact information was verified against the source. The phone number '(877) 623-6748', email 'ej@ncdenr.gov', and URL 'https://deq.nc.gov/permits-regulations/title-vi-compliance' are perfectly captured as they appear in the source document.

The output contains all text sections present in the single-page source document, including the 10 divisions list, the language assistance information, the list of considerations for translation, and the Title VI compliance notice.
There are no tables in the source document to evaluate.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The text within each paragraph and the bulleted list has been captured in its entirety. No significant omissions of sentences or key phrases were detected.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output maintains high fidelity to the source wording. Minor reformatting such as making the technical assistance paragraph separate from the lead agency statement is acceptable and improves reading flow for structured data.

checks.a_analysis: The content in the output faithfully represents the source document without any duplication. The text from the highlighted box (regarding technical assistance) is placed in its logical reading order following the heading, and is not repeated elsewhere in the document.

The document contains a single heading which is assigned level 2. While starting at H2 is slightly non-standard, there are no skipped levels or incorrect nestings. The level adjustment falls under acceptable accessibility normalization.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, no table structures were corrupted in the conversion.

The reading order in the JSON follows the logical flow of the PDF. It correctly identifies the logo first, followed by the main blue heading, the text within the green highlighted box, and then the subsequent paragraphs and list. This matches the intended visual and informational flow.
The unordered list containing the four criteria for providing translation services is correctly identified and structured in the JSON output, with all list items preserved.

All critical links found in the source document, including the Title VI compliance URL and the contact email address, are correctly captured and hyperlinked in the output. The URL is correctly reconstructed from the line-broken text in the source.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization of the source PDF. The sequence of information—from the logo and lead agency statement to the technical assistance callout (originally in a green box), followed by the divisions list and language assistance policies—is preserved in the correct logical and visual order. While the green background for the technical assistance section is replaced with bold text, this is a standard styling adaptation for HTML and does not impact the visual flow or meaning of the document.
The source document does not contain any tables; the content consists of headers, paragraphs, and bulleted lists.

The primary meaningful image, the DEQ logo (comprising the North Carolina map outline and 'DEQ' text), is present and rendered correctly in the HTML output. The colorful decorative row of hands at the bottom of the PDF is absent from the HTML, which is an intentional accessibility improvement as per the provided instructions, as it serves a purely decorative purpose and contains no informational content.

There is no visual duplication of body content. Each paragraph, list item, and contact detail appears exactly once in the sequence corresponding to the source document. The appearance of the document title at the top of the HTML and within the logo/header area is a standard accessible design practice and not a content duplication defect.
